observations.recommendation: Management adhere to the pertinent provisions of the Local Government Code and cause the Sangguniang Bayan to enact updated ordinances that can be the basis of the general revision of real property assessment and classification once every three (3) years to maximize lgu’s effort in raising local revenues.

Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit the corresponding reports to the Office of the Auditor immediately thereafter. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.
observations.reason: 2019 Reiterated as Finding # 1 in this Report.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial statements and DVs and other accounting reports. The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to distribute equitably work assignment among accounting personnel so as to prevent work overload and be able to meet deadlines on report submission.
observations.reason: 2020 Reiterated as Finding # 7 in this Report.

observations.recommendation: Management should require the General Services Officer/ Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS. Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fie and other contingent event in accordance with Republic Act No. 656.

We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical
inventory of its property, plant and equipment. Items that are beyond economic
repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.

We also recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and to
conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to ensure correctness.

We recommended that:

a. the Municipal Mayor constitute a committee to conduct inventory of all road
networks of the Municipality;

b. the Municipal Mayor fill-up the position of Supply Officer;

c. The Supply Officer maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map and
keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for each network;

d. the Municipal Engineer provide the local accountant and the Supply Officer
with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for
road projects.

e. The Municipal Accountant ensure that the transfer all local roads to the
account Road Networks Account shall be completed and keep Local Road
Network Property Ledger for each network.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a committee to undertake the
reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS
on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a
commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Budget Officer to maintain
the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation for each class of
expenditure, as prescribed, to ensure the preparation of the Statement of
Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and
establish a strong internal control on obligation of expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials to
submit to the Audit Team copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete
supporting documents, within five days from perfection thereof, for timely review of
the legal and technical aspects, reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate
the inspection of the projects.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to
institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered
sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis,
planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that:

a. The Municipal Treasurer strictly adhere to the guidelines pertaining to the
submission of and disbursement vouchers and other accounting reports.

b. The Municipal Accountant distribute equitably the work assignment among
accounting personnel so as to prevent work overload and backlogs.

Management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment and determined the carrying accounts and recognized the depreciation charges and impairment losses in accordance with the PPSAS 17 and 27 respectively. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly dropped from the books

observations.recommendation: The management should instruct officials concerned to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with Section 122(2), P.D. 1445.

Management should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest

Recommendation:
a. We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical
inventory of its property, plant and equipment. Items that are beyond
economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.
Management must adhere the PPSAS 17 & 27 of Volume I and II
respectively.
b. Management must create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the
overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the
office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
c. Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE,
as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets
and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint
custodial responsibilities.
d. Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger
Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and
likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect
complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
e. Require the accountable officers of the LGU to update the physical
inventory of PPE in adherence to Section 63, Manual on the NGAS, Vol.II
and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the
Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
f. Every accountable officer must adhere and carefully evaluate their
accountability on Section 494,GAAM, Vol. I, which states that “Every
accountable officer for government property shall be liable for its money
value in case of improper or unauthorized use or misapplication thereof, by
himself or by any person for whose acts he maybe responsible. He shall
likewise be liable for all losses, damage or deterioration occasioned by
negligence in the keeping or use of the property, whether or not, it be at the
time in his actual custody (PD 1445).

Recommendation:
We recommend that copies of POs/Contracts and AIR be submitted on time to the
Auditor as required under Section A(1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and
Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Recommendation:
Management should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of
unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS and Pag-Ibig,
Zamboanga City on the interest due. Thereafter, they should come up with a
commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those
officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contribution should be
answerable for their actions.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Accountant and Treasurer be instructed to
strictly adhere the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial statements
and DVs and other accounting reports. Instruct the head of the accounting section
to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work
overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions
and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting
Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.
Request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned
personnel until such time that the requirements of the COA are being complied
with Section 122(2), P.D. 1445.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management should require the Municipal
Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require
insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS.
Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due
to fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No. 656,
otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245 dated
July 13, 1973.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under   of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) fast tract the implementation of the projects/programs funded by the National Government Agencies so as not deprive further the public of the use and benefits derived from the said projects and follow-up to the concerned NGAs the immediate release of funds.

We recommended that management prepare its 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan with emphasis on the compliance of the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations. Prepare annually the action plan on solid waste management that details the priority activities to be performed and delivered for the gradual implementation of its 10-year plan.

We recommended that management prepare its Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) and Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP) which may lead to unfavourable impact socio-economic and environmental  development as provided under Section 106, 107 & 109  of R.A No. 7160, Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that to provide information for a complete profile of the Leagues of Local Government Units (LGUs) and the Leagues and Federations of Local Elective Officials pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2011-014 dated December 13, 2011 and COA Memorandum No. 2012-008.

We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment.  Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.

LGU must submit and furnished copies of contract and memorandum of agreement and their required attachment within five (5) days from their issuances to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommend that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommend that municipal accountant and the municipal treasurer be instructed to strictly adhere the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial statements and disbursement vouchers and other accounting reports. Instruct the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.

LGU must submit and furnished copies of contract and memorandum of agreement  and their required attachment within five (5) days from their issuances to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommend that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment.  Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books. Management must adhere the PPSAS 17 & 27 of Volume I and II respectively.
Management must create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities.
Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
Require the accountable officers of the LGU to update the physical inventory of PPE in adherence to Section 63, Manual on the NGAS, Vol.II and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
Every accountable officer must adhere and carefully evaluate their accountability on Section 494,GAAM, Vol. I, which states that “Every accountable officer for government property shall be liable for its money value in case of improper or unauthorized use or misapplication thereof, by himself or by any person for whose acts he maybe responsible. He shall likewise be liable for all losses, damage or deterioration occasioned by negligence in the keeping or use of the property, whether or not, it be at the time in his actual custody (PD 1445).

We recommend that municipal accountant and the municipal treasurer be instructed to strictly adhere the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial statements and disbursement vouchers and other accounting reports. Instruct the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

Management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance   Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) fast tract the implementation of the projects/programs funded by the National Government Agencies so as not deprive further the public of the use and benefits derived from the said projects and follow-up to the concerned NGAs the immediate release of funds.

We recommended that management prepare its 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan with emphasis on the compliance of the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations. Prepare annually the action plan on solid waste management that details the priority activities to be performed and delivered for the gradual implementation of its 10-year plan.

We recommended that management prepare its Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) and Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP) which may lead to unfavourable impact socio-economic and environmental  development as provided under Section 106, 107 & 109  of R.A No. 7160, Local Government Code of 1991.
10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan with emphasis on the compliance of the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations. Prepare annually the action plan on solid waste management that details the priority activities to be performed and delivered for the gradual implementation of its 10-year plan

We recommended that to provide information for a complete profile of the Leagues of Local Government Units (LGUs) and the Leagues and Federations of Local Elective Officials pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2011-014 dated December 13, 2011 and COA Memorandum No. 2012-008.

We recommend that the accounting office immediately compute or consider the existing computation based on the prior years for the depreciation of the PPE based on the available records.   Thereafter, an adjustment should be made after a physical count is completed.
